channel.title: SCP Foundation - new forum posts
channel.description: Posts in forums of the site "SCP Foundation" - Secure, Contain, Protect

item.title: Re: Seeking my first critique!
item.content:encoded:
gotcha! I've been working on the idea to make it a bit more interesting and current, so I hope you'll like the changes I've made.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Seeking my first critique!

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Deletion Vote
item.content:encoded:
ii


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5547

item.title: Re: Staff Post- Deletion Vote
item.content:encoded:
Third senior staff voting for deletion and confirming that this page meets deletion criteria.

☑ ☑ ☑


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5127

item.title: Staff Post - Deletion Vote
item.content:encoded:
Beginning deletion vote at -11.

If you are not the author and you want to rewrite this article, you may reply to this post asking for the opportunity to do so. Please obtain permission from the author (or the Rewrite Team if this article is older than 6 months) and make sure you copy the page source to your sandbox. Please do not reply to this post for any other reason unless you are staff.


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5547

item.title: Re: Seeking my first critique!
item.content:encoded:
It's not unique enough. Maybe loose the, "sad to be alive," part. I don't mind giving a sentient anomaly a personality but this is fairly common and uninteresting. "A small cube of flesh," isn't very descriptive. Go into more detail. Is it a perfectly smooth cube or does it have pertrusions? Is it just a hunk of flesh that produces a liquid? This idea suffers from a lack of originality.You should make the discovery more interesting. Instead of being found in a hospital scheduled for demolition, why not being found by police in the basement of a cannibal? Maybe it was created from the remains of one of his former victims? I'm not saying thats exactly what it should be, but think of something more unique, something that will surprise readers.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Seeking my first critique!

item.title: Forum Criticism Team - Captain Post
item.content:encoded:
25Airships, when giving feedback in this forum, try to provide critique that is specific to the concept rather than vague and interchangeable between any post.

What about the concept isn't interesting, and why? What part of the premise needs to be explained better? Is it an issue of needing more detail or writing with better clarity?

For more information on giving feedback in this forum, please see this thread: http://scp-wiki.wikidot.com/forum/t-9804362/so-you-want-to-be-a-concept-reviewer


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: The Modification Shop

item.title: Re: SCP Tabletop RPG
item.content:encoded:
Of course!


Forum category: General Discussion / Fan Work
Forum thread: SCP Tabletop RPG

item.title: Re: The Modification Shop
item.content:encoded:
It's an okay idea but not very interesting. Try to explain things a bit better as well.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: The Modification Shop

item.title: Seeking my first critique!
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article
Genre : Emotional

Elevator Pitch: A small cube of human flesh was 'born' in an abandoned maternity ward. It exudes emotional control over those near it, affecting one Foundation agent quite radically.

Central Narrative: Agent Vega, an operative on the scene during initial containment, becomes obsessed with taking care of the cube, and perceives it as a human baby, and as his own biological son.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: The cube feeds on being cared for like a baby, and is not necessarily malicious on its own. The cube isn't sentient and the actions of weeping and crying like a child are just biological impulses it has to survive. Ultimately, the SCP has no feelings and just wants to live, on the very base level that simple mammals do.

Additional Notes: I don't have anything of particular worry but I am excited for folks to take a look! I have my sandbox ready to go whenever someone wants to take a look.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Seeking my first critique!

item.title: [SEEKING GREENLIGHTS] for SCP idea
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre: I'm not sure

Page Layout: A normal article, with several addendums

Elevator Pitch: The inhabitants of the world split into two factions and are about to get into a war. The player could stop it, since being the protagonist of the game the inhabitants of it treat him like a celebrity.

This presents a dilema to the Foundation: Should they interviene? If the people inside of the game are to be considered human, should the Foundation policy of political neutrality apply?

Central Narrative: A cartridge of Pokémon HeartGold where every NPC is sentient and self-aware. Every inhabitant of the world lives a life on their own, without knowing they are inside of a video game. The player cannot really communicate with them (since that's not an option in Pokémon games), but the Foundation can monitor their society by talking to the NPCs, whose dialog changes regularly.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: I think a mini Pokémon world living inside a cartridge is a pretty good concept which hasn't been tried before.

Additional Notes: I feel the article lacks a central narrative to tie it all together. I may need help with that.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: [SEEKING GREENLIGHTS] for SCP idea

item.title: Re: Requesting Greenlight for SCP (Your Chance to Win)
item.content:encoded:
All-in-all, this seems really predictable. It really just operates like a normal quiz show, outside of the petty vandalism. The anomalies don't add anything interesting to that baseline.

With these kind of SCPs, it can often help to think about the "personality" of the SCP, or at least what it "wants." On this read-through, it looks like they just want to run a normal quiz show. You could dig further into that, push what this being/operation is, what they want, and how they try to get it. There are a lot of directions you could go from there (making them sympathetic, making them really alien, etc.), but it'll take a lot of further development for this to go anywhere.

Compare this to something like SCP-2030, which only has the basic form of a TV show. (There's a really good breakdown of what makes SCP-2030 unique and effective in this post)


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Requesting Greenlight for SCP (Your Chance to Win)

item.title: [Seeking Greenlights] SCP Concept: I do not have a body
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Page Layout (Optional): Because of reasons specified in further sections, the article would be written solely in headings, images, and footnotes; that is to say, no body text.

Elevator Pitch: A cloud of massless, blue gas, around the size of a yoga ball, with two small discs of lighter color that mimic the appearance of cartoon-like eyes. It can move approximately walking speed in any direction, hear, understand, and speak English, and can “see” through its “eyes.” Whenever it possesses something (whether by taking it or by being given it, whether it is aware or not) that is considered to be a “body” by an unspecified dictionary (Merriam-Webster), that thing disappears.

Central Narrative: This SCP claims to have originally been a human that was “cursed.” It acts benevolently towards humans, and it voluntarily turned itself in to the Foundation. It was found that the SCP would not willingly take the body of a human (living or dead), and would have to be forcefully “given” it for the body to disappear (i.e. “you can have this human” would not work, but “this human is yours” would). Further, if someone said “this body now belongs to the SCP,” even if the SCP was out of earshot and unaware of the body’s existence, the body would disappear. The dictionary-bound nature of the SCP was then discovered. One test found that when it was given the “flavour of this wine,” the wine became tasteless. Partway through testing, the SCP began to feel lethargic. It was found that the dictionary’s council was considering adding an additional definition to its page that would include the SCP. This was swiftly prevented, but it made the Foundation aware of a killswitch. After sufficient testing was conducted, the containment procedure was decided; a small orbital craft with 2-way audio connection. If the signal is disrupted for too long of a time, it is to be assumed it has breached containment and will be terminated immediately. Further, its existence is kept to be top-secret, and all involved personnel without sufficient authorization have been administered amnestic.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: Unlike most overpowered villains, it has interesting limitations and abilities. Since this SCP is able to cause most things to disappear, it is incredibly dangerous. However, this same ability makes it very powerful if controlled by the foundation. It can also only to be used as a last-ditch effort, though. For example, the unexplained disappearance of SCP-682 would definitely cause some alarm and raise some questions. Thus, the Foundation would be inclined to keep it alive, but only as a last resort.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: SCP Concept: I do not have a body

item.title: Re: Finding the Scarlet King's children.
item.content:encoded:
Where is this said?


Forum category: SCP Universe / Foundation Universe
Forum thread: Finding the Scarlet King's children.

item.title: Re: What about a Canadien Site?
item.content:encoded:
The sites are based on language, not nationality. English-speaking Canadian users would be most at home on -EN, while primarily-French-speaking Canadians would likely be more comfortable on -FR. There is absolutely no reason to institute a "Canadian Branch" when the major official languages1 are covered already.

There is nothing stopping someone from writing Canada-based content here, on -FR, or, indeed, on literally any of the wikis.

Footnotes
1. Can't speak for the First Nations' languages. If someone fluent wants to create a site for those, that'd be neat.

Forum category: Site Announcements and Proposals / Proposals And Policy
Forum thread: What about a Canadien Site?

item.title: The bad omen man
item.content:encoded:
Greenlight: Possibly

Page type: Scp Article

Genre: Fantasy

Elevator pitch: The bad omen man is an unfortunate anomaly that seems to break everything around him against his will. He is kind and witty, but with a short temper.

Central Narrative: The bad omen man is a tall black humanoid, with their facial structure resembling a bat. When bored the structures around them accelerate deterioration by about 10 fold, resulting in poor foundation and containment breaches. When upset, things tend to happen like breaches with no logic of how it could have happened, sudden personnel death, and in one case, the detonation of the internals of a generator causing a site-wide blackout and breaches. This is not restricted to any radius around him, happening all over the world, and even on other planets, and close realities. Fortunately, if given time, he will bond with personnel. He enjoys video games, board games, and honestly is a very polite and kind individual, despite his short temper.

Hook/Attention getter: Entertainment is a good thing, but sometimes it's the most important thing.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: The bad omen man

item.title: Forum Criticism Team - Captain Post
item.content:encoded:
Geirundi, to better help the author here, can you be more specific? What about the idea do you think is great? Why?

Why do you think it needs to be fleshed out? You've given some examples, but you don't really provide any reasoning for why the author might want to add those elements. What do you as a reader want to see developed further, and why? How would that improve the article?

For more information on providing feedback in this forum, please see this thread: http://scp-wiki.wikidot.com/forum/t-9804362/so-you-want-to-be-a-concept-reviewer


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: The Broken toys

item.title: Forum Criticism Team - Captain Post
item.content:encoded:
Author, per the required reading forum guidelines: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-13282684/required-reading:how-to-use-this-forum-ideas please fill out the concept template to seek feedback on your idea.

Remember to please edit your previous post using the "edit" function under the "options" tab to the lower right of your first comment rather than creating a new thread.

Once you have revised your post accordingly, please reach out to reviewers to request that they look at your thread. You can find a list of ways to contact reviewers in this thread: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-13282692/how-to-get-more-reviews


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: SCP idea seeking greenlight/help!!!

item.content:encoded:

Remember to please edit your previous post using the "edit" function under the "options" tab to the lower right of your first comment rather than creating a new thread.

Once you have revised your post accordingly, please reach out to reviewers to request that they look at your thread. You can find a list of ways to contact reviewers in this thread: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-13282692/how-to-get-more-reviews

The story is really nice (no not only because I'm biased and that I think that the CotBG is the best GoI imo), and apparently I'm to dumb and can't translate the "real" Name of Bumaro into Linear B. Could ya give me a lil hint ;D

Re: Okay maybe I have it now, is it
+ show block
– hide block

Hephaestus cause he's a smith and u know Bumaro smithes a lot I guess


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: The Battle of Baikal

item.title: Forum Criticism Team - Captain Post
item.content:encoded:
VoxStulti, if you don't meet the criteria to give greenlights, please don't confuse authors by posting "non-official greenlights". Given that staff have encountered authors who have mistakenly thought that any review given counted as a greenlight (even if the reviewer gave a negative review), the less confusion in the forums, the better.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Chearleader Pom Poms[Seeking Greenlight]

item.title: Re: The Broken toys
item.content:encoded:
I think this a great idea with a lot of potential, maybe just flesh it out a little, maybe there's a certain instance where it came from, or maybe something else, or maybe they're just angry toys, who know, I think if you fleshed it out more, it would be a great outcome


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: The Broken toys

item.title: Re: Seeing page comment replies
item.content:encoded:
It doesn't have ANY? How do people have conversations here?

I personally check the Recent Posts listing, but you can also look at the "recent posts and comments" section of your wikidot profile, or bookmark the threads you're currently waiting on replies for. Or ask people to please send you a PM when they've replied.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Questions Desk
Forum thread: Seeing page comment replies

item.title: Forum Criticism Team - Captain Post
item.content:encoded:
Author, per the required reading forum guidelines: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-13282684/required-reading:how-to-use-this-forum-ideas please fill out the concept template to seek feedback on your idea.

Remember to please edit your previous post using the "edit" function under the "options" tab to the lower right of your first comment rather than creating a new thread.

Once you have revised your post accordingly, please reach out to reviewers to request that they look at your thread. You can find a list of ways to contact reviewers in this thread: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-13282692/how-to-get-more-reviews


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: end of the world, just you and me -Tale Idea

item.title: end of the world, just you and me -Tale Idea
item.content:encoded:
Warning, there might be potentially triggering content here. I'm an amateur writer so I don't know if I can handle these topics very well yet.

Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: Tale

Genre (Optional): Drama/Emotional

Page Layout (Optional): Typical story structure (maybe a choose your own adventure short?)

Elevator Pitch: end of the world scenario, 2 people survive, one of them decides that living in an empty world of just pain isn't worth it and decides to end their own life. The second person after living with the other for so long is crushed by this and either goes to recover from this trauma or is impacted by this so heavily that she loses the will to go on.

+ show block
– hide block

Central Narrative: GD-Class Gestalt Dissolution Scenario occurs

90% of the population vulnerable without a specific immunity gene is turned into eldritch primal hunters, extremely dangerous with varying anomalous effects ranging from instantly causing simulated 3rd degree burns to resurrecting the dead as mindless slaves. All of which with diminished intelligence and fight each other regularly in tribe-like primitive collections, leading to them becoming sparse in population without method of copulating. Self destructive in nature, although powerful.

The story is set around 2 people, a man and woman. They are happy to have each other around in this apocalypse, they survive for months through continuous hardship fighting/avoiding these beings. But the man is becoming more and more tired, he figures that life will never become better, every second of every single day at every inconvenience he just wants it to be over. To him, this could be a tempory problem, but what if it never gets better? What is the point of living through this? Everyday involves pain with no reward in sight. The man decides to give himself to one of the hunters right in front of the woman.

so there's 2 ways this ends, tell me which one sounds better:
a) the woman is depressed for a while but then recovers stronger from the death of her friend, moving past it and continues with the hope of a better future

b) the woman is completely broken by the death of her friend and falls into a depression adopting the hopelessness of the man and is mauled by the hunters

I also have an alternate setting: humanity is obliterated, there is barely anyone still living. The man and woman are alone and that sense of loneliness without hope of a better future. The man, living with depression since he was very young only has it amplified in this bleak setting and he dies, not from monsters but by his own volition.

Which leaves the woman alone, without anyone left alive. The only person who kept her company dies from something completely out of her control.
a) she keeps going and finds someone else
b) she never finds anyone else and continues surviving by herself in a castaway situation in the company of a backpack with a face drawn on it.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: The end of the world scenario in of itself as a starting point and the interesting relationship between these two in this new world would be a good way to introduce the story. Their daily lives and their battles and the like, before arriving at the climax to make it impactful.

thank you for reviewing my idea! I know it has a lot of problems but I'm happy to hear feedback.


Got it, thanks zyn mate, I'll keep in mind those mobile users next time
Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: end of the world, just you and me -Tale Idea

I'm glad to finally see the continuation of Josephine and Benjamin's journey. Really good stuff Blue.

+1


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: The Mekhanics of La Rue Part Two

item.title: Re: On the Shores of Paradise
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the recrit!

Here are some responses in return!
Wooooooooosh

In other words, what aspect of this concept do you want to focus on?

If I had to choose, the first part (the panic at the Site) would be my choice of focus. I feel the sense of mystery would be useful to keep readers driven to read and find out the solution. Driving force = mystery, panic, and ethical arguments between P1 & P2.

And as another possible answer to your initial question (lessening of emotional impact), how about this, which happens as P1 is being rescued:
- MTF is sent to rescue P1, and, as per her their1 her instructions, the MTF members are equipped with gas masks
- The MTF walk into the town, which is covered with plants (which slowly try to wrap around their legs)
- After 15-20 mins they find her in a house (following her shouts and gunfire) strung up by large amounts of vines and plants, being dragged out of the window
- P1 is freed by the MTF members, who all begin to walk out of the town
- However, the gas masks, which seem effective at first, begin to fail as the thaumically charged gas begins working its way through the filters
- When they are five minutes from making it out of the town the masks lose most of their effectiveness, and the MTF members begin hallucinating various things (fears, the town free of plants, their members dead, etc.)
- They have to stop and get P1 to direct them verbally via radio
- This initial explanation is interrupted by a large burst of light, followed by a strong gale coming from the gateway (this is the hive mind consuming the people’s thoughts, which creates a huge amount of thaumic energy that subsequently charges the plants covering the town)
- As the MTF and P1 puzzle over this, the plants around them begin rapidly growing and blooming
- P1 panics and tells the MTF members to run, hand in hand to not lose any members, while a member contacts the waiting team and asks for an immediate evacuation, which will arrive in 5 minutes
- The MTF team tells her that she has to direct them out, as they can’t trust their vision, and P1 shakily agrees
- Basicalky, the log follows P1 as she desperately tries to direct the MTF members and prevent their deaths
- Inevitably, she isn’t able to save a couple of them
- In the end, they are airlifted away as they watch the plants bloom and spread in a beautiful and horrifying display
-
I’m really unsure about this one, but may serve as an emotional continuation to the Site (massive pressure placed upon P1 to save her saviours, and she is unable to fully do so). This may suffer from the same problem as my last suggestion, so please tell me if it does.


Anyway, thanks for reading, and hopefully this is suitable!
Footnotes
1. I’ve typed ‘her’ so many times, only to correct it to their/they, that I’m giving up on correcting it. Her it is.

Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: On the Shores of Paradise

item.title: Re: suadeant (seeking greenlights)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you so much for your assesment! For the record i am completely on the same page with everything you wrote here. Should i develop this more? I was under the impression that would be a wasted effort until after it had been greenlit. Especially since i got told that my post is already a "long" post.

Most interesting was your point about not being noticed yet needing to communicate with people. I really think i can polish that into a very interesting dynamic and a limitation for the entity itself, something like relying on our tendancy to dismiss the unexplainable. My idea is that it works through something akin to the idea of "inception"

Over all, very helpful review! I assure you that pretty much all of your thoughts have been considered and are being expounded on! I would be happy to discuss the finer points of each of your statements any time if you are interested and would love to reveal some of the things that i have in store ^^


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: suadeant (seeking greenlights)

item.title: Re: The Modification Shop
item.content:encoded:
It sounds like it works too well, we tend to thrive on malfunctions. The invisible force that prevents customers from leaving when they haven't paid may be an interesting hook. Maybe focus on the D-classes that couldn't pay and are stuck inside.

You may want to read SCP-212 which is arguably similar or SCP-2904 which is another SCP that seems to be beneficial until it turns out to have major drawbacks that make it less useful that anticipated. I hope those give you some ideas on what to do with this.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: The Modification Shop

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Deletion Vote
item.content:encoded:
Second senior staff voting for deletion and confirming that this page meets deletion criteria.

/ /


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5134

item.title: Re: Bicephalic(SEEKING GREENLIGHTS)
item.content:encoded:
But why? I mean, clearly an anomalous creature, but what's it's story? You don't have to eplain where it came from(it might help though), but you do need to tell us what it is doing now and why we should care that it is in containment. I mean, two snake heads is wierd, but if it's not dangerous, and it's not doing anything else, why do I care? This is actually pretty boring, but if you can come up with something interesting, like one always lies and the other tells the truth(kinda been done, but still more interesting than this) or they're constantly scheming to bite each other off, or they represent some dichotomy like Cain and Able or The Broken God and the Sarkics, then maybe it's interesting. I'm not able to give greenlight, but after struggling to get a greenlight myself, I'm quite certain this isn't enough for the people that do have the power.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Bicephalic(SEEKING GREENLIGHTS)

item.title: SCP Idea: the music box
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes
Page Type: Article
Genre: Horror
Page Layout:
+ show block
– hide block

Lyrics (of the song SCP-XXXX plays)
Item Number, Object Class
Containment Procedures
Description
Exploration Logs
Any addendums needed

Elevator Pitch: It is a music box made of painted blue brass with a handle also made of brass. Opening the music box shows a figurine of a young female student. The mechanism is the reverse of a non-anomalous music box, the handle needs wound for the music not to play. Any sentient being that hears the song will go into an REM sleep and dream of a school, in which they are hunted by a humanoid entity

Narrative:
D-Class find a cassette tape players in the locker they wake up by
Throughout the school, cassette tapes are found, revealing what happened to the school
Last exploration, D-Class finds files on a dangerous student before being killed.
(Planned theme: How certain people are left behind - will be revealed through tapes and files)

Hook: The weird setup for this SCP. The idea is for the reader to ask questions to encourage them to read further.

Additional: The “narrative through exploration logs” is what 087 and I’m afraid this will just be a 087 ripoff.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: SCP Idea: the music box

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.content:encoded:
Hi
I'm new to the site and decide to join after wanting to hopefully one day write a successful scp article! My account name is Codey Finn, and right now id love some good o'l swiss roll cake…

item.title: Forum Criticism Team - Captain Post
item.content:encoded:
Since this thread was on page 6 of the forum listing with no responses, this is a quick staff reminder to the author that if you haven't yet, you should contact reviewers directly to request feedback as needed.

Please refer to this thread for more information: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-13282692/how-to-get-more-reviews

If you have already reached out to multiple reviewers and received no reply, notify a staff member for assistance finding a reviewer.

item.title: Forum Criticism Team - Captain Post
item.content:encoded:
Author, please mind the Ideas Critique required reading wordcount limit; your post is about 190 words over. Please address this before seeking further feedback.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Seeking Greenlight: Cloning a Thaumiel Human?

item.title: Re: The Laughing Tree
item.content:encoded:
Hi Red,

Going along with your suggestion: What if I change the purpose of the interview? Instead of an investigator, the interviewer is a Foundation psychologist available to personel and their families. The parent brought their kid in because he/she was worried for their well-being but needed an expert. Both adults do not believe the kid at the start of the interview, but slowly grow more inclined to the idea. This might then prompt a small investigation into the names on the tree and reveal its root system, eventually leading to the final interview.

How will you plan to show all this in the narrative?

So at the momment, there are three "sources" of information: The child, the camp staff, and the small investigation. For simplification, I've laid out tables below of where each point might come from. Please note that I want the kid to be aware that the tree is doing all this.

Child Int socially isolated frame for theft tree trips kids kid wet himself kid feels shame
Small Inv Staff name written on tree names = anti-social Names unremovable root network aversion to urine
Camp Staff Int Staff were in on it staff former victims The Tree writes the names Isolated victims are common victims laugh at pain

If any of the above seem out of place, or a piece of data is missing, please let me know.

Best,
Granite


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: The Laughing Tree

item.title: Re: Mind blown
item.content:encoded:
<3


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: The Moloch Mentality

item.title: Re: If this needs to be Top Secret, then...
item.content:encoded:
I've got kind of this idea in my head that it was created as a guest book for some sort of anomalous bed and breakfast, and it records not just the reader's name, but everything they did since they had their last REM cycle, and continues recording until either they have another, or they die. Maybe throw in a comment about how a majority of the book is taken up by the story of a person who was on anxiety or anti-depressant medication that stopped them from going into REM sleep?

I like the idea of it simply recording things, rather than compelling you to read.

The foundation could even have ways of getting the information out by having a D-class with an extremely common name wakeup and immediately begin photocopying pages, and then go back to sleep before being allowed to interact with any foundation personnel?

There's a lot you could do with the idea. It's a shame the article was so sparse and didn't go into any of the possibilities.


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5134

item.title: Staff Post - Deletion Vote
item.content:encoded:
Beginning deletion vote at -12.

If you are not the author and you want to rewrite this article, you may reply to this post asking for the opportunity to do so. Please obtain permission from the author (or the Rewrite Team if this article is older than 6 months) and make sure you copy the page source to your sandbox. Please do not reply to this post for any other reason unless you are staff.


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5134

item.title: Requesting Greenlight for SCP (Your Chance to Win)
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre (Optional): Semi-horror

Page Layout (Optional): SCP Class (Euclid), Containment Protocol, Description, (Possibly Picture)

Elevator Pitch: SCP-XXXX is a radio tower built somewhere around the 1980s around Ballard, Utah. It has not been fed power since its abandonment somewhere between 1985 and 1990. The tower broadcasts a radio signal which is still able to be picked up by radio receivers approximately six miles in the surrounding area.

Central Narrative: Every three to four days, SCP-XXXX broadcasts a signal in which a excited voice is heard announcing something (email, last few credit card numbers, social security number, home address, license etc.) to identify a seemingly random individual. Other than these occurances, only static can be heard when tuned to the channel. (This is bizarre due to the lack of power being sent to the tower)

Hook/Attention-Grabber: Within the least recorded timeframe of a few hours to the most of roughly a year, an entity (deemed SCP-XXXX-2) visits the individual previously announced on the radio transmission. This entity is dressed in a formal attire with a black suit, pants, and shoes with a white undershirt and purple tie. SCP-XXXX-2 tells the person that he is from a reward group and that the person has been chosen to have a chance at winning "glamorous prizes" and requests to sit down with the individual. In the case that the subject accepts, SCP-XXXX-2 expresses apparent gratitude and sits down with them. Once seated, he asks the subject questions ranging from trivia, to multiple choice. If the subject answers correctly, SCP-XXXX-2 leaves their sight and disappears. The following day, the subject receives gifts such as new kitchen equipment, cars, furniture, decor or gift cards. In the case that the subject declines, SCP-XXXX-2 shows frustrating, discomfort and disdain towards the subject. As the same as the previous case, leaves the subject's sight and disappears. The exception to this case is that immediately (30 seconds) to later (days to weeks) something damaging happens to the subject's house. (chandeliers coming loose and falling, to kidnapping/home invasion)

Additional Notes: Please rate the professionality of the article and the overall idea. Thank you.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Requesting Greenlight for SCP (Your Chance to Win)

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.content:encoded:
Hello!

I'm Comrade_rusky, i've been a fan of SCP for a few years now, i've played the games and read the posts and finally felt it time to contribute! Also I love the whole SCP theme of secrecy around creepy government stuff.

For the question of the month, I would say a nacho cheese plate to share.


Forum category: Site Announcements and Proposals / Introductions
Forum thread: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers

item.title: If this needs to be Top Secret, then...
item.content:encoded:
Might I suggest the book revealing personal data about the opener — the kind of information a skilled private detective would uncover doing the same amount of field work? (One hour reading about yourself = a one-hour hunt for clues.) The book "resets" when it changes hands, so it couldn't be used to grab someone else's personal secrets directly. However, another person could persuade/compel the holder to read the contents out loud while a third person took notes. With this much potential for blackmail, no wonder the Foundation wants SCP-5134 locked away from lower-clearance personnel!


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5134

item.title: Re: Gossip Ghost
item.content:encoded:
I think the idea of a close minded gossip ghost that's slowly going mad in the data space is…both horrifying and surprisingly hilarious.

I actually kinda want to see you make it seem spiteful and silly at first before the inevitable dive into afterlife-based madness. Make it funny. Before making it horrifying. Either way, this concept is dope enough for a second GREENLIGHT.

Feel free to PM me for draft crits down the road. Go get 'em, pardner! 👍


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Gossip Ghost

Been waiting a long time for this, and I'm glad how it turned out!

+1


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: The Mekhanics of La Rue Part Two

item.title: Re: The Interdimensional Bodega
item.content:encoded:
Howdy hey!

+ Line by line and general thoughts.
- ...

Edits/SPaG/Whatever

SCP-XXXX-1 is to be staffed at all times by Foundation personnel, with at least one taking on the role of a concierge.

the word staffed here is a bit weird, but I'm unsure what you would replace this with.

No entrants are to be permitted to leave.

"No entrants are permitted to leave."

The use of amnestics on entrants is strictly forbidden.

"The use of amnestics on entrants is strictly prohibited"

It is only visible to individuals who have been granted access to SCP-XXXX-1; those with access to SCP-XXXX have been estimated to comprise of 20% of the population.

Are you talking about the two different things? Like SCP-XXXX-1 vs SCP-XXXX?

SCP-XXXX’s location varies, although it is consistently found on flat surfaces in locations with low amounts of human activity. Examples include:

Those examples include places with a lot of human activity. Floors, Back Alleys, cardboard boxes are normally where people are.

Individuals who can see SCP-XXXX are granted access to SCP-XXXX-1; access to SCP-XXXX-1 is done by cupping a hand over SCP-XXXX as well as on the surface on which it is located.

"Individuals who can see SCP-XXXX are granted access to SCP-XXXX-1. Access to SCP-XXXX-1 is done by cupping a hand over SCP-XXXX as well as on the surface it is located."

How this information was obtained is currently unknown.

"It is currently unknown how this information was obtained."

SCP-XXXX-1A and SCP-XXXX-1B are bipedal avian creatures with features similar to owls (Strigiformes) and both standing at approximately 2 meters.

"SCP-XXXX-1A and SCP-XXXX-1B are bipedal avian creatures with features similar to owls (Strigiformes) and standing at approximately 2 meters."

//<Meyers slings her rifle over her shoulder.>

Missing ending //

After 24 hours you will default to the Handcuffs.

But isn't it the canteen in the Specials containment procedures?

final notes

Hmm, this is alright. I dislike how the addendum was structured. I would have broken them into separate addendums, though I think that's a bit more of a nit pick. I dislike the explanation of the handcuff object, it just seems really cheesy and just weird in context.

Other than that, I did enjoy this and I think y'all have done good with this. Though, I do suggest getting more critique before posting.

Good luck!


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Drafts Critique
Forum thread: The Interdimensional Bodega

item.title: Re: Sword Ballerina (Seeking Greenlight)
item.content:encoded:
If I don’t make the SCP interesting, it would look like a tale, not a SCP article.

Why not make it a tale?

I didn’t decide between any of these and maybe another solution would be better.

Pick whichever one you feel would be most fun to write about. I personally would recommend not writing something reliant on another anomaly, though.

All things considered, with the level of minute details you want to include, I think a Serpent's Hand GoI format would make more sense for something like this. Have you read any of those kinds of pages?

ETA: received a response via PM

I'll respond here, as is something short and not really an answer. I've never taken an interest in the GoI, so I've never read those articles. I'll read some of the Serpent's Hand as you indicated. I'm also thinking about separating the idea into smaller pieces, like a tale about the host AND a SCP article, a GoI format and a tale or something like this. I'll mature the idea by reading some GoI before changing it.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Sword Automaton (Seeking Greenlight)

Added the calculator that always answers 666.


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: Log Of Anomalous Items, Vol III

item.title: Re: Sword Ballerina (Seeking Greenlight)
item.content:encoded:
I think I've addressed everything you said by now in the edits of the first post. I’ll try to respond to the last reply’s topics here, but if the previous one still has loose ends, let me know.

Unless you've got some additional requirements to slap on that require "honorable combat" or the like, which just feels even more cliche and contrived.

I felt exactly this when I first wrote the SCP idea. I really don’t want this “honorable combat” thing, you can outnumber it or make an ambush. I think the last answer serves this question as well.

+ show block
– hide block

So, the SCP has this method of separating the wheat from the chaff: It doesn't react every time. Non-humanoid entities, diseases, accidents and other threats it considers that the knowledge it contains or a good warrior can surpass easily are ignored by the sword. It only reacts if the warrior is off guard as a last resort, e.g. the host is sleeping or the host can’t see the threat. If the “threatful”-”threathless” line feels strange, please help me with that.

My question is why. You've already got a lot of kind of random details, abilities, and restrictions that don't really match for the automaton; why does it need to be a backup file too?

So, this is connected to the “Ultimate Warrior” thing. It backups things that it understands that help the host to be more experienced in combat, which includes war memories. This connected to SCP-3041 someway, one of this SCP inspirations, as erasing the subjects memories doesn’t make it really forget what happened as the SCP “feels” that it is necessary.

I say pick one to focus on and remove most of the minutiae from the other.

I think I’ll pick the SCP, but it’s a difficult decision. If I don’t make the host interesting enough, the narrative is worthless as you don’t really care about them. If I don’t make the SCP interesting, it would look like a tale, not a SCP article. What I’m trying to do is showing the SCP effects and story using the host narrative as an exemple. Anyway, I would like some guidance here.

If you don't have anything planned for why this automaton exists the way it does, just say that. If you do have a backstory for why it acts this way, tell the reviewers so we can let you know if we think it's a good story or not.

Putting it on the lines of “Have you decided on a proper background for the SCP?”, there are two answers:

Short one: No.

+ Long one: It's complicated…
– hide block

You see, I had thought of a lot of stories about that, but felt unsure about all of them. The SCP has a lot of inspiration in a greek myth: the Palladium myth.

The Palladium is a wooden statue that was created by Athena, the Goddess of War, in homage to Pallas, her best friend who she accidentally killed during a training. The cities where the Palladium was guarded were protected by its power, Troy and Rome were secured by it while they had it secure.

Based on it, I’ve created some different backgrounds around it:

  1. It is an essophyssical representation of the word “War”, “Sword” or “Warrior” that was sealed on a gladius by another ontokinectic entity. I’ve dropped this soon as I perceived it as a weak narrative.
  2. It is Pallas herself transformed into a sword. She is interested in reuniting war-based info to have revenge on Athena. I’ve lost interest in this one because it makes most of the things confusing, such as the protection, the memory keeping and the two forms.
  3. It was created by mycenaean greek witches to protect a warrior, but it went wrong as its directive was “protecting the most powerful warrior and helping them to grow stronger”. I still like that, but it is flat and needs to be developed. Who are the witches? Who was the warrior? Why have they done that?
  4. It is a thaumaturgical device that will achieve godhood once it becomes the real “Ultimate Weapon” of the “Ultimate Warrior”, with every information about fighting and surviving. I could not think of an “why” for this one.
  5. It is an alien device to store human warfare info, designed as a simple weapon that the aliens thought could be used by every human, a sword. As it wants to know how it would be the “ultimate human warrior”, it always keeps company to who has the title (by its standards). I’ve given up on this one because it makes the “protection” thing confusing.
  6. It is the “sibling” of another weapon SCP and it's trying to create the perfect host to overcome its sibling. I didn’t like this one as I’m afraid to touch other people’s work. I also thought that it would maybe make the SCP weak by itself.

I didn’t decide between any of these and maybe another solution would be better.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Sword Automaton (Seeking Greenlight)

Overall, the core anomaly and general narrative are far too dull and short-lived to be enjoyable. The anomalous attributes exhibited by SCP-4273 aren't detailed particularly well, and from what I can get out of the description, what it does do isn't very interesting; something more of an AI (anomalous item) than something that would be given its own SCP № designation.

As for the narrative, it's quite bland in all honesty. An individual in power binding their soul to an object to maintain power even after death isn't very original; bordering on a cliché at this point. Furthermore, even if the narrative was something of grand uniqueness and intrigue, the article itself is far too short to convey much meaning and makes it hard for me to have any notable feelings for what's going on here.
-1


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-4273

item.title: Re: The Oreads
item.content:encoded:
Additionally, if they are mountain nymphs… why are they on the beach and made of sand? I believe there are sea nymphs, so why not make them those? I just think there's a disconnect between the resulting anomaly and its origins.

Can't mountains erode from long and powerful exposure to water?

I think the idea of this anomaly being aggressive with people isn't necessary for it to be interesting… it could have been discovered because someone went on the beach and shit started forming out of the sand around them in response to their thoughts… I find the idea that it was ever aggressive a little underutilized here.

That can be changed. I only put that in to demonstrate the sands ability to act on it on. It's better if this is addressed later now that you've said it. What if instead, it was discovered by a group of bird spotters instead? Since the park the beach is in is a wildlife reserve protected by the UNESCO, it would make sense that the bird spotters found the beach and its effect by accident and reporter it to the authorities, who then went to UNESCO which drew the attention of the Foundation.

+ Long Idea Here
- IDEA!!!

So I am a sucker for Greek Myth, and this isn't something grandiose like Zeus or Hades or whatever, so I dig that. But my concern is that here is a premise for a story that revolves entirely around these creatures being something of myth. There's no twist, nothing to do the unexpected… and there doesn't seem to be much of a personal connection. You mention w

What if I kill to bird with one stone here. How about this? The Foundation discover the sand can understand Koine Greek. After this revelation, the foundation investigates further and the sand drops more and more hints that suggest that it's The Oreads. But, later, when the head researcher begins experimenting with attempting to give the sand back it's true, individual form, a change occurs. The sand drops a comment and says it's unfamiliar with the form the head researcher is attempting to give them and describes their true form as something much more alien and monstrous.

The Foundation begins looking it to this and further comments from the sand begin to suggest that it's actually an otherworldly1 being rather than the actual mythological nature nymph. The Foundation then discover that the sand may have initially been hostile to humans in the past and the fight with the SCP was conveniently beneficial to the human. This would explain why the sand can understand Koine Greek. They don't speak our language. We're speaking theirs. All Hellenic languages are in fact non-human in origin.

As a result of this, the Foundation decides to suspend all experiments into returning the sand to its true form and instead opt to simply contain it and pretend that they're looking for the sand other parts when in actuality, they're trying to stop it from ever reuniting.

Does that sound better or is it too tropy? It changes the hook from sad to sort of panicked.

Footnotes
1. I'm not sure whether their alien, monster or some subspecies

Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: The Oreads

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hello all. I forgot to do my introduction post a few weeks ago when I joined, but now that I'm starting to critique articles and some of my own drafts are getting close to completion, here I am.

My friends introduced me to SCP, and I had forgotten about it until a few months ago when my ADHD decided I would hyperfocus on it. That's good for my writing though, as most of my spare thoughts are about my article ideas, so I have hours every day to revise them in my head.

If you can help with my grammar/clinical tone, go right ahead.

I'm just planning to do some critique and edits before I post any of my own original ideas.

As you can see here, I tend to ramble a lot since I type really fast. Could use some help making my articles more concise.


Forum category: Site Announcements and Proposals / Introductions
Forum thread: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers

item.title: Re: The Interdimensional Bodega
item.content:encoded:
All in all, i like the concept and the flow, the last dialog is what felt the least natural, if that makes sense, here are the thoughts that i got as i read the piece:

+ Bullet-pointed critique
- Bullet-pointed critique
  • Who are the entrants? (Answered later in the article, but felt kinda lost about their purpose/identity until it was answered, not sure how i'd tell you to fix this, or if it needs fixing, but felt it was important enough to mention)
  • The text looks like hebraic symbols, is that intended to be a easter egg or a connection to something or another?
  • "currently unidentified runes" isn't there a more 'clinical' term for that? Something along thaumaturgical anomalous symbols or something, grain of salt here, i'm still grasping that whole clinical terminology aspect
  • "Because you boarded up the last one, jackass…" Could change jackass to (expletive) but that's a nitpick i guess
  • Containment Log 1D: You're repeating information from the containment attempt on the foreword, i think it isn't necessary. Specifically, "Attempt at convicing the employees to relocate SCP-XXXX-1"
  • Also, if it's invisible to some and visible to others, how does that translate to recorded media? Do the rules still apply? The footage description implies that anyone can see it when recorded, which if true, means you could have an AR setup to look for them with VR googles and a camera, and if that's the case, does that imply people recording random things with cameras/cellphones could see them when they shouldn't?
  • "SCP-XXXX-1B: Dude, we found that one like two seconds after finding yours. Much better place to be honest. We’ve traded on the London Stock Exchange there." Does that mean the unveiled earth is better for them? It felt that way from this line.
  • Containment Log 1E, the containment attempt part is empty, unless that's on purpose, but it looks like a typo.
  • "to help you make your decision, but in 24, hours" < That last comma is a typo i think
  • I understand the meaning behind the punchline, but with the infodump on the last dialog, it feels like you're hitting the point a smidge too much. It can work either way, however.
  • Moreover, the timejump between negotiation and figuring out the choice feels "rushed", as if they managed to find it by means of epiphany. I think it would help to clarify how they got to this specific solution, maybe the Wandsmen have had this happen before, and had a similar setup? Something along that. I can understand how they'd have reached that particular solution however, so if you want to keep it for the sake of brevity, i could understand that reasoning.

Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Drafts Critique
Forum thread: The Interdimensional Bodega

item.title: SCP-Idea: Museum Of Reminiscence
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greelight: Yes
Page Type: SCP Article
Genre: Drama
Page Layout: Standard SCP Article

Elevator Pinch: Anomalitic properties happen when an individual remains inside the Museum for more than 30m, then the individual perceives the reality changing until the interior of the Museum is completely new, at this stage the reality inside the Museum is completely connected with some memory of the individual, normally the memory is deep and has great importance for the individual. It is important to say that if there is more than one person inside the Museum, the anomaly will manifest a memory that connects all individuals in the Museum. If by chance there is no connection between all individuals then the anomaly will not happen.

Central Narrative: the story involves the researcher who is doing the article as he will participate in the story as well. The researcher in question has a troubled past and the experiment in which he is the guinea pig itself will be the most important part of the SCP

Attention-Grabber: Hmmm I think that is the first problem I have. I dunno if I have really a unique thing with my SCP. This is my first attempt anyway so will put my Additional Notes here because if anyone have an Idea of some "Special Flavor" to put in my SCP than I would be thankful.

Anyway that is my SCP Idea. Quite a simple one if you ask me.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: SCP-Idea: Museum Of Reminiscence

item.title: Re: Translation
item.content:encoded:
You're allowed to seek feedback on our forums, I believe. Other members of staff feel free to correct me.

Anyways, you would be better off getting feedback on the INT Wiki rather than here or the French branch. The INT Wiki also has a discord you can join for faster feedback. Once you're done you can choose to post either here or on the INT wiki.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Questions Desk
Forum thread: Translation

item.content:encoded:
